title: Talking About Artists
author: MintFace
excerpt: Eric Bleicher (Line 312) updated his Line. Three years of growth, three surfaces for collectors — and a masterclass in artist presence.
content:
Eric Bleicher (Line 312) dropped me a DM last week saying he had updated his Line. I had to revisit immediately. Eric's curated gallery was one of the wildest I discovered his line in a digital walk back in late 2022. He was one of the first to move beyond the presentation of art in a single line, by placing his tokenized artworks in the digital heavens, reflecting the subject matter; Northern Lights.

3 years brings plenty of growth for artists, and many are now updating their Lines and websites to reflect that growth…and I'm totally here for it.

"This is an ongoing list of my available tokenized work on Ethereum, beginning with my most recent mints (2026) and working backward to my genesis collection, which was created in the summer of 2021." — EBleicher

Now Bleicher has three experiences for potential collectors:

  1. The traditional artist website (ebleicher.com/blockchain)
  2. The virtual exhibition (The Line 312)
  3. The artist profile (The Line Artist Profile)

These experiences create a surface of exploration and education for collectors before hitting the marketplace. Each experience is evaluative in nature… getting to know the artist, their visual language, how they have curated their work, the narrative they speak to the world and what they are trying to say about themselves and the world around us.

Five years of growth is a substantial evolution for artists early in their careers so we are getting to see this firsthand and tokenized for the very first time.

"Since beginning my blockchain journey in 2021, I have sold 30 1/1s, 48 editions, and a split 1/1, encompassing 62 holders & 5.095 eth in volume. I have also airdropped 25 out of a possible 30 editions to holders of 1/1s from my first two collections (both minted in 2021) — the final five are airdropped to the purchasers of the remaining 1/1s from those early mints." — EBleicher

The Line creates an immersive intensity from the aurora lit skies, so that we are looking upwards virtually in real awe of each lens based click. Experiential worlds are complementary to the traditional artist website that allows for a breadth of material beyond the blockchain.

The Line artist profile is a signpost to all three surface areas. Explore the virtual world and be nudged further into Bleicher's nighttime reality… explore the latest mints on his Line artist profile… or exit via his traditional artist website. Entry points create options…and experiences shift perceptions.

However you are juggling the demands of creating and distributing, Eric's approach of many different forms of presence is one that is easy to admire. Take a leaf out of his tokenized book and work on giving your own Line a glow up. 5 years of creating is worth celebrating.

Yes of course there is a X Article and thread worth reading too :)
